- Last updated: `2026-05-28 21:36 UTC`
- Current request: investigate why watchlist/algo signal bubbles are not hydrating after the restart.
- Current status:
  - Live `/api/signal-monitor/matrix` does return usable bubble states. Example: ASML returns `2m/5m/15m` states, including a fresh `5m` buy row.
  - A 3-symbol matrix miss took about `13.4s` and the frontend scheduler only requests `3` symbols per pass under normal pressure.
  - Root cause found: the frontend signal-matrix scheduler always sliced the first refresh-needed priority symbols. After market bars stop advancing, already-hydrated symbols became refresh-needed again based only on `latestBarAt`, so the first few priority rows could monopolize the tiny request batch and later bubbles appeared unhydrated.
  - Fixed by rotating refresh-needed priority symbols fairly and using `lastEvaluatedAt` as part of the refresh freshness anchor so hydrated rows do not immediately re-enter the missing queue just because the latest completed market bar is unchanged.
  - Replit startup config untouched; no processes killed.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`

  - `artifacts/pyrus/src/features/platform/signalMatrixScheduler.test.js`

  - Passed: `pnpm --filter @workspace/pyrus exec node --import tsx --test src/features/platform/platformRootSource.test.js` (`53` tests).
  - Passed: `pnpm --filter @workspace/pyrus run typecheck`.
  - Passed: `git diff --check`.
- Next step:
  - Let Vite pick up the frontend change through the running Replit app. Bubbles should hydrate progressively across the priority/watchlist set instead of repeatedly refreshing the same first rows.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 21:23 UTC`
- Current request: investigate why Signals-to-Action now shows `Awaiting next scan / Signals appear as soon as the monitor finishes its next pass` with a critical warning, and why rows disappear between scans.
- Current status:
  - Root cause found and fixed in backend signal-options state generation.
  - Live `/api/signal-monitor/state?environment=paper` had fresh monitor states (for example ASML/DIA), but `/api/algo/deployments/7e2e4e6f-749f-4e65-a011-87d3559a23b0/signal-options/state` and `/cockpit` returned `signals: []` and `candidates: []`.
  - Cause: `isSignalOptionsActionableSignalState` and `isSignalOptionsActionableSignalSnapshot` hard-capped Signals-to-Action rows at `barsSinceSignal <= 1`. Signals still marked fresh by the monitor (profile `freshWindowBars: 8`) disappeared from the action table after one bar, so the table showed `Awaiting next scan` between fresh-new-signal moments.
  - Fixed by relying on the monitor's `fresh === true` state plus direction/signal timestamp instead of a separate one-bar cap.
  - The critical warning seen in the UI is separate but related runtime status: cockpit attention reports `gateway-readiness` critical because the market session is closed for algorithm execution, while diagnostics also report resource pressure from a long signal-options scan/API latency.
  - Replit startup config untouched; no processes killed.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 21:04 UTC`
- Current request: investigate and fix the repeated Pyrus `Maximum update depth exceeded` root crash without bandaid removal.
- Current status:
  - Root cause investigation found multiple real render-loop triggers during startup, not one generic tooltip problem.
  - Confirmed one concrete invalid-tooltip call site in `HeaderStatusCluster.jsx`: `header-ibkr-line-usage` was an `AppTooltip` inside the outer IBKR header button. It now uses native `title`/`aria-label` so Radix no longer mounts a tooltip trigger inside that button.
  - Kept `AppTooltip` on Radix for safe non-interactive triggers, but added guards so interactive/composite triggers fall back to native title instead of Radix ref composition.
  - Fixed the `AlgoScreen.jsx:771` crash: signal-options candidates fell back to a fresh `[]` before queries resolved, so the selected-candidate cleanup effect could dispatch every render. Startup fallback arrays are now stable and null resets are idempotent.
  - Fixed the `PlatformApp.jsx:2529` crash: signal-monitor state fell back to a fresh `[]`, feeding an unstable signal-matrix universe; the signal-matrix pruning effect then committed a fresh snapshot even when no states changed. Signal-monitor fallbacks are now stable, and signal-matrix state updates no-op when the merge output is equivalent.
  - Live headed browser verification against the reported Replit URL loaded the app without `Maximum update depth exceeded`. Boot reached `100%`; remaining console errors were backend `/api/gex/*` 503s, not React crashes.
  - Replit startup config was not touched. No repo-defined workflow/startup files were edited.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 20:29 UTC`
- Current request: fix delayed Massive equity/spot signals and watchlist sidebar signal bubbles not hydrating/refreshing in realtime.
- Current status:
  - Root cause for Signals-to-Action lateness was Massive signal-matrix bar freshness plus stale action-row surfacing: 5m signal bars could be accepted 10-15 minutes behind, and action rows could include stale monitor states. Fixed Massive realtime signal-matrix gap-fill tolerance, restricted action rows/scans to current or one-bar-old signals, and made signal-options state/cockpit active-screen routes.
  - Root cause for watchlist signal bubbles was client matrix scheduling: symbols were treated as hydrated forever after first fill, the foreground priority list included the full active watchlist, and the universe included all saved watchlists. Under `watch` pressure this could put visible sidebar dots behind a long rotation.
  - Fixed watchlist bubbles by rehydrating stale matrix states based on `latestBarAt`, passing `nowMs` into the scheduler, scoping the matrix universe to selected/visible/open-position/recent-monitor/active-watchlist symbols under pressure caps, and keeping foreground priority to selected/visible/open-position/recent-monitor symbols while the rest rotates in background.
  - Direct backend matrix probe confirmed Massive can return fresh matrix bars when asked: latest `2m 20:22`, `5m 20:15`, `15m 20:00` at `2026-05-28T20:22Z`.
  - No app processes were killed/restarted, and Replit startup config was not touched.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 20:13 UTC`
- Current request: user challenged the prior aggregate-universe cap/OOM assumption and asked whether higher memory headroom means we were artificially limiting the app and degrading flow scanner potential.
- Current status:
  - User was right to challenge the memory assumption. The live container reports a 16GB cgroup limit (`/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.max = 17179869184`), memory.current around 7.4GB during inspection, memory.peak around 10.4GB, `memory.events` with `oom 0`/`oom_kill 0`, and no memory PSI pressure.
  - Root cause correction: the app was not near container OOM at ~2GB API RSS. The old hardcoded RSS pressure thresholds (`watch 900MB`, `high 1200MB`, `critical 1600MB`, hard block 3000MB) were artificially forcing `scanner-throttled-high-pressure` and effective concurrency `1`.
  - Implemented corrected pressure model in `resource-pressure.ts`: RSS pressure now scales from the actual cgroup memory limit unless env overrides are set. On this 16GB Replit container the thresholds resolve to watch/high/critical `4096/5734/8192 MB`, with hard block `11469 MB`.
  - Kept API heap pressure strict and independent; the change only corrects RSS/container pressure so Node heap stress can still throttle when real heap pressure exists.
  - Revised the Massive aggregate universe cap so it is not an artificial default constraint: `MASSIVE_STOCK_UNIVERSE_STREAM_DEFAULT_SYMBOL_CAP` is now `1000`, while `MASSIVE_STOCK_UNIVERSE_STREAM_SYMBOL_CAP` remains available for an intentional operator cap.
  - The raw Massive quote firehose remains disabled because that was an event/CPU firehose issue, not a memory-capacity issue.
  - No app processes were killed/restarted, and Replit startup config was not touched.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 20:18 UTC`
- Current request: refine the Pyrus loading-policy implementation because the full-screen boot overlay improved, but visible containers/panels still show waiting after entering the app.
- Current status:
  - Root cause for this follow-up: the previous loading change conflated "do not block boot" with "do not start until boot is complete" for watchlists/accounts/signal-profile. That moved some waits from the full-screen overlay into visible header/rail/panel containers.
  - Additional chunk-path finding: the Market chart panel lazy-loads `MultiChartGrid`, which then pulls charting/runtime chunks. The prior preload began only after the Market screen was already visible, so the visible chart container could still show a chunk loader.
  - Implemented refinement: non-blocking data queries now start immediately again, but the boot overlay still does not wait on unrelated data for screens that do not need it.
  - Implemented refinement: Market chart module preloading now starts once as soon as the Market screen module evaluates in the browser, before the visible panel effect runs.

Once pressure moved to high/critical, scanner diagnostics flipped to `scannerFillMode: pressure-throttled`, `topLimitingReason: scanner-throttled-high-pressure`, and effective concurrency `1`.

- Current request: identify what still needs to be fixed after Milestone 1, run a soak/monitor to find the runtime cause, and investigate why the lower-corner app readout is not showing the readiness/pressure state while the app runs.
- Current status:
  - Root-cause pass complete. The 10-minute soak ran against the currently running pre-patch API process without killing or restarting any intentional sessions.
  - Soak summary: 40 samples from `2026-05-28T19:31:54Z` to `2026-05-28T19:41:40Z`; API CPU avg `95.57%` (max `97.6%`), RSS avg `1822.5 MB` (max `1930 MB`); readiness was `not_ready`/critical in 37/40 samples; line usage timed out in 5/40 samples.
  - IBKR lines were not the blocker: `flowScanner` stayed `0`, scanner fill was `pressure-throttled`, and `scannerBlocked` stayed `null`. Broker trading readiness stayed `ready`; app readiness was blocked by `api_resource_pressure_critical`.
  - Runtime root cause confirmed through `/api/diagnostics/runtime`: the options-flow stock support path starts a Massive full-universe stock quote/aggregate stream for about 550 symbols. The stream had produced roughly 967k quote events within minutes and kept the Node API main thread/RSS saturated.
  - Signal lag is real, not only a UI readout issue: signal samples showed latest bars 8.45-14 minutes stale and latest signals 14.43-56.44 minutes stale while profile/state evaluation lag was much lower. Re-check after the stream-shedding fix is live.
  - Lower-corner readout root cause: `FooterMemoryPressureIndicator` was driven by the client memory-pressure hook and did not carry server dominant drivers such as `api-rss` into `pressureDrivers`; the mini API bar also preferred API heap over API RSS. That made the live footer fail to show the actual backend pressure driver the user was looking for.
  - Implemented source fixes: the API now closes/skips the Massive full-universe stream under high/critical API pressure, and the footer now surfaces server pressure drivers plus an `API RSS` mini readout when RSS is dominant.
  - Important caveat: the currently running API is still the old build. Source tests/typechecks/build pass, but live acceptance needs a normal Replit Run App restart/reload before the post-fix soak.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 19:12 UTC`
- Current request: pick up the dropped scanner line-allocation investigation from this stable session; determine why 192 free lines remained idle with `scannerFill: blocked`, `backgroundBlockedReason: resource-pressure`, `topReason: critical-scanner-shed`, and `flowScanner: 0`, while conserving weekly usage.
- Current status:
  - Using session-handoff and root-cause investigation workflows.
  - App/API runner is currently stopped from terminal-stability cleanup; no fresh runtime probes have been run in this resumed pass yet.
  - User corrected that multiple Codex sessions are intentionally running. Do not terminate or clean up other terminals/process groups unless the user explicitly authorizes a specific target.
  - Prior runtime evidence showed the scanner allocator was not filling because resource pressure was `critical`; pressure drivers included API RSS around 2 GB and API latency/dominant slow route p95 around 30s.
  - Root cause confirmed in code: the scanner hard-shed gate used raw `resourcePressure.level === "critical"`, so RSS-only critical pressure around 1.6-2.0 GB forced scanner concurrency to `0`, blocked queueing with `resource-pressure`, and released flow-scanner leases even though heap pressure was not critical and live lines were otherwise free.
  - Fix implemented: added `isApiResourcePressureHardBlock()` so RSS-only critical pressure below 3 GB is diagnostic/throttling pressure, not a scanner shed. Critical heap pressure and severe RSS still hard-block scanner gates.
  - Scanner diagnostics now report RSS-only critical pressure as `scannerFillMode: pressure-throttled` with effective concurrency `1`, instead of `blocked` with concurrency `0`.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 19:00 UTC`
- Current request: investigate why Codex/Replit terminals keep dropping back to shell and conserve remaining weekly usage.
- Current status:
  - Scope corrected: the user is reporting terminal/Codex session drops, not just app preview disconnects.
  - No cgroup OOM kill evidence: `/sys/fs/cgroup/memory.events` has `oom_kill 0`.
  - Repeated pattern observed: after a terminal appears to drop to shell, the prior Codex process often remains alive as the foreground process group on that PTY. Retrying/resuming starts another Codex process, stacking duplicate sessions and burning CPU/usage.
  - Stopped app runner/API/Vite pressure from the earlier investigation; this reduced cgroup memory from ~6.7 GB to ~3.6-3.8 GB.
  - Killed duplicate detached Codex process groups on `pts/0` and `pts/2` multiple times. Current clean state at 19:00 UTC: only active Codex is this session (`pts/3`, PGID `70686`); `pts/0` and `pts/2` are plain shells.
  - Codex log search did not show quota/weekly-limit failures. It did show duplicate startup sessions and one shell snapshot validation error: `Shell snapshot validation failed ... syntax error near unexpected token '('`.
  - Recommendation while unstable: do not launch/resume Codex in the other terminals; each retry can create a live duplicate session and spend usage.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
- Validation state:
  - Confirmed no OOM kills.
  - Confirmed current process tree has only one Codex session after cleanup.
  - App dev runner is stopped; restart only via Replit's default Run Replit App when app validation is needed.
- Next step:
  - If another terminal drops, capture `ps -eo pid,ppid,pgid,sid,tty,tpgid,stat,pcpu,pmem,rss,etime,comm,args --sort=-pcpu` before retrying Codex. If duplicate Codex remains foreground on a dropped PTY, terminate that PGID before opening another agent session.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 18:49 UTC`
- Current request: fix the observed IBKR line-usage bug where scanner work was active but using `0` flow-scanner live lines, and address Replit workspace/terminal lag.
- Current status:
  - Root cause found: scanner live hydration called `fetchBridgeOptionQuoteSnapshots()` with `releaseLeasesOnComplete: true`, so flow-scanner live quote leases were admitted and then immediately released after the snapshot call. This produced UI states like active option-chain scans with `Flow Scanner active = 0`.
  - Fixed scanner live hydration to keep admitted flow-scanner quote leases until TTL expiry, explicit rotation/preemption, abort, or error.
  - Updated the scanner regression test so it now asserts flow-scanner live leases remain admitted after quote snapshot completion.
  - Rebuilt the API bundle after the source fix.
  - Lag investigation found multiple stale/non-current Codex terminal process groups consuming CPU. Terminated stale groups and left only the active session (`43439`/`43446`) running.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 18:35 UTC`
- Current request: implement the IBKR 200-line saturating scheduler plan for live/historical options data lines, account/position monitoring, algo exploration, scanner rotation, pressure normalization, and read-only diagnostics.
- Current status:
  - Implementation complete for this pass.
  - Market-data admission now defaults all live pools to the bridge-reported usable budget, normally 200, and treats scanner lines as a saturating rotating working set instead of a fixed 80-line lane.
  - Protected execution/account/algo position-mark demand and visible foreground demand preempt scanner rotation; scanner demand fills remaining live quote capacity and rotates same-priority scanner owners.
  - High API pressure throttles scanner concurrency without zeroing scanner lines; critical pressure explicitly sheds scanner-only live option leases while leaving foreground/account/execution/algo position marks allowed.
  - Scanner diagnostics and `/api/settings/ibkr-line-usage` no longer shed leases or reconcile prewarm leases as a side effect. Admission diagnostics filter expired TTL leases from active counts without expiring them.
  - Hybrid scanner hydration is restored: historical option bars provide broad/shallow discovery, and selected candidates receive live option quote hydration for bid/ask/last/greeks/freshness.
  - IBKR line-usage observability now reports portfolio counters for protected/pinned lines, priority lines, scanner rotation lines, scanner move-ready lines, pressure limiting reasons, historical work, and bridge drift.
  - Settings line-usage UI normalizes and displays the new portfolio counters.

- Current request: validate the recent work and investigate the reported PYRUS root crash in Account positions.
- Current status:
  - Runtime reachability was rechecked: web `/` on local `18747` returned `200` in ~15ms; API `/api/healthz` returned `200` but slowly (~4.3s). Diagnostics showed the app reachable but API/browser health degraded from slow routes.

  - Fixed the API typecheck issue in flow-scanner historical option hydration by normalizing generic bar market-data mode strings before copying them onto IBKR option-chain contracts.
  - Updated options-flow high-pressure tests to match the accepted pressure behavior: high pressure throttles scanner concurrency but does not block radar/background work; critical pressure still blocks and sheds.
  - Isolated option-chain unit tests from live env/test-state drift by resetting historical admission, disabling durable market-data store reads inside the suite, pinning delayed Massive recency, and making immediate cache-invalidation refetch deterministic.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 16:50 UTC`
- Current request: audit the Accounts page, especially the P&L calendar, for variance in recent prior data and today's data.
- Current status:
  - Audit completed. The variance is real and comes from source/period mismatches rather than only stale rendering.
  - The P&L calendar uses dedicated 1Y equity-history and unfiltered 400-day closed-trade queries, then prefers NAV-derived daily total P&L when a baseline is available and overrides the current market date from account summary `dayPnl`.
  - Finding 1: today's calendar value is overwritten with account summary `dayPnl`, but backend summary `dayPnl` is sourced from open-position quote change (`IBKR_POSITIONS` / `QuoteChange`) instead of the NAV-derived total day P&L used by the calendar. Live sample for `U24762790` at `2026-05-28T16:47Z`: NAV-derived current-day calendar value was about `+225.68`, while summary override forced about `+202` to `+212.60` as quotes moved. A combined-account probe was worse: summary `dayPnl` was `0` while NAV-derived current-day value was about `+225.68`.
  - Finding 2: the Today intraday panel requests `range: "1D"`, which is a trailing 24-hour backend range, and then uses the first returned point as the baseline. Live `1D` history began at `2026-05-27T16:50Z`, so the Today panel can plot yesterday-afternoon-to-now instead of today's market session.
  - Finding 3: the calendar rolls weekend activity to the next weekday only, not the next market trading day. The live recent series put a `-52.77` NAV move on `2026-05-25`, which was Memorial Day/NYSE holiday handling territory; the app already has NYSE full-holiday logic in `features/charting/marketSession.ts`, but the account calendar model does not use it.
  - Existing Account page files are dirty from prior account-stream/prewarm work; preserve those changes and avoid unrelated edits.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
- Validation state:
  - Passed: `pnpm --filter @workspace/pyrus exec node --import tsx --test src/screens/account/accountPnlCalendarModel.test.js src/screens/account/accountCalendarData.test.js src/screens/account/IntradayPnlPanel.test.js`
  - Live API comparison completed against `http://127.0.0.1:18747/` for `combined`, `U24762790`, and `U24947962`.
- Next step:
  - Fix the source contract: derive account/page "today" P&L from same-day transfer-adjusted NAV where available, stop overriding calendar current-day totals with quote-only `dayPnl`, make Today intraday use market-day/session points, and use market holidays for account calendar bucketing.

  - Scanner later blocked correctly under the resource-pressure gate:
    - `16:36:06Z`: pressure `high`, `backgroundBlockedReason: resource-pressure`, flow-scanner lines 0, effective scanner concurrency 0.
    - `16:36:55Z` onward: pressure `critical`, scanner remained blocked with zero flow-scanner lines.
  - Remaining line owners during critical pressure were transient/small automation/visible leases, mainly `algo-operations:CRWV` using 2 automation lines until it released by `16:38:28Z`.
  - Diagnostics endpoint timed out several times even after scanner line leases dropped, so there is also non-line API/IO pressure.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 16:03 UTC`
- Current request: review/audit the Algo control-area UI/UX work, starting with option strike selection supporting multiple selections per side and the selected DTE control needing better scale/redesign.
- Current status:
  - First audit slice completed; no production code changed for this request.
  - Findings:
    - Strike selection is a real single-choice contract, not just a UI limitation. `SignalOptionsExecutionProfile.optionSelection` exposes scalar `callStrikeSlot` and `putStrikeSlot`; `ContractSelectionCell` reads those scalar fields and renders two radiogroups. Backend selection/fallback helpers also start from one preferred scalar slot per side.
    - The existing source tests assert the current single-select behavior (`aria-checked`, radiogroups, scalar `patchProfileDraftPath(field.path, Number(slot))`), so tests must change with the UX.
    - DTE controls are visually fragmented: min DTE + 0DTE lives in a small `ContractDteCell`, target/max are generic compact number cells, and the `DteWindowRail` renders a fixed 0-90 day scale. Current default 1-3 DTE compresses into the first few percent of the rail, making selected DTE hard to read.
  - Recommended implementation path:
    - Treat multi-strike selection as a schema/runtime change, not a front-end-only toggle. Add array fields such as `callStrikeSlots` / `putStrikeSlots` with backward compatibility from scalar fields, then update API automation/backtest/profile resolution before changing the UI.
    - Redesign DTE as one full-width control: prominent target value, editable min/target/max steppers or compact inputs in one row, 0DTE as a scoped toggle, and a local scaled rail based on the active window rather than fixed 0-90 for every profile.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
- Validation state:
  - Source audit only; no production tests run.
- Next step:
  - Implement the schema/runtime-backed multi-strike selection and DTE control redesign in a focused pass, then update focused source tests and run Pyrus/API typechecks plus targeted signal-options tests.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 15:28 UTC`
- Current request: propose a better way to track and persist reasons for sudden Replit/Codex dropped sessions.
- Current status:
  - Reviewed the current `session-handoff` recovery rules, Replit artifact startup command, `artifacts/pyrus/scripts/runDevApp.mjs`, API diagnostics persistence, and diagnostic DB schema.
  - Existing lifecycle evidence is useful but not durable enough for full project/container restarts: the supervisor writes `/tmp/pyrus/pyrus-dev-lifecycle-8080.jsonl`, which disappears with the container.
  - Existing API diagnostics persist to Postgres and are useful after the API starts, but they do not capture pre-API supervisor boot state, supervisor child exits before API readiness, cgroup/kernel counters at the supervisor layer, or next-boot classification of the previous run.
  - Recommended direction: add a low-overhead app-owned "flight recorder" that writes a rolling heartbeat and restart incident summary to durable workspace storage first, optionally mirrors to Postgres after the API is healthy, and classifies the previous run on next boot.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 14:33 UTC`
- Current request: run the live PYRUS app in-browser and diagnose page/container/element rendering delays, specifically treating `Loading algo monitor` / `Waiting for the active section to finish first paint` as symptoms rather than root causes.
- Current status:
  - Ran live Playwright timing audit against the existing app at `http://127.0.0.1:18747`.
  - Confirmed root rendering-policy issue: frame-owned Algo monitor data is gated by `frameAuxiliaryDataEnabled`, which currently requires the active page's `backgroundAllowed`; several pages only set `backgroundAllowed` after deeper body/derived sections are ready.
  - Measured examples:
    - Boot Market: frame/sidebar visible at ~845 ms; Market anchor visible at ~9.5 s; Algo monitor still `Preparing` at 20 s because Market `derivedReady/backgroundAllowed` stayed false. Warmup showed `memoryPressureLevel: "critical"` and chart-grid readiness never opened the background gate.
    - Flow: frame visible at ~313 ms; Flow anchor and `backgroundAllowed` at ~17.1 s; Algo monitor only moved from `Preparing` to `Loading` after that gate opened.
    - Account: account screen anchor visible at ~772 ms; critical ready at ~1.8 s; still `Preparing` at 20 s because Account `derivedReady/backgroundAllowed` stays false until derived stream or the 35 s derived fallback.
    - Algo: algo screen anchor at ~1.8 s; `backgroundAllowed/frameAuxiliaryDataEnabled` opened at ~7.2 s; sidebar then moved to `Loading`.
  - Code evidence:
    - `PlatformApp` makes `frameAuxiliaryDataEnabled` depend on `activeScreenBackgroundDataAllowed`.
    - `PlatformShell` makes `algoFrameRuntimeEnabled` depend on `frameAuxiliaryDataEnabled`.
    - `PlatformAlgoMonitorSidebar` renders `Preparing algo monitor` when `dataEnabled` is false.
    - `MarketScreen` sets `backgroundAllowed` only when `chartGridReady` is true.
    - `AccountScreen` sets `backgroundAllowed` only when `accountDerivedReady` is true, and derived fallback is `35_000 ms`.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
- Validation state:
  - Completed live browser timing audit. No code changed yet for this issue.
- Next step:
  - Decouple frame-owned sidebar/runtime data from active page deep `backgroundAllowed`; use critical/frame readiness for frame data and reserve derived/background gates for true page-owned background work.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 14:27 UTC`
- Current request: watch whether the live algo is properly picking up signals coming from the Massive market data API.
- Current status:
  - Live audit confirms provider/session state is Massive-backed: `/api/session` reports `marketDataProvider: massive`, Massive stock websocket was active, and the signal-options cockpit reported `signalSourcePolicy: massive-primary`.
  - The algo is picking up Massive-backed signal state: deployment `7e2e4e6f-749f-4e65-a011-87d3559a23b0` is enabled, `lastEvaluatedAt` advanced during observation from `2026-05-28T14:28:07.490Z` to `2026-05-28T14:31:49.640Z`, and cockpit showed latest signal bars through `2026-05-28T14:20:00.000Z`.
  - Latest observed signal list included fresh Pyrus signals such as DIA buy at `14:10`, KTOS buy at `14:00`, COIN/HOOD buy at `13:50`, and COHR/CIEN sell at `13:45`; no newer signal than `14:10` was observed.
  - Important limiter: diagnostics/cockpit reported critical resource pressure. Signal refresh was allowed, but heavy/action work was deferred and position marks/action scans were capped; API process was pinned near 100% CPU and ~2.5 GB RSS, causing `/api/algo/events`, cockpit, and detailed state probes to time out intermittently.
  - Replit startup config has been locked with `pnpm run replit:config:lock`.
  - Scope remained observation/audit; no algo code changes were made.

  - Source check: signal-options code and regression tests still pin the signal source policy to `massive-primary` / non-`ibkr-only`.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 14:20 UTC`
- Current request: implement the PYRUS app rendering-policy fix after auditing why page bodies wait while the frame/header/sidebar render instantly.
- Current status:
  - Fixed the readiness boundary: the shell no longer marks a screen ready just because its host/fallback committed.
  - `screenRegistry` now marks a screen ready only after the real screen component is loaded, visible, and committed; hidden unloaded screens return `null` instead of hidden loaders.
  - Navigation now mounts the target screen synchronously through `activateScreen`, so `setScreen` cannot point at an unmounted screen until a later effect catches up.
  - Added a delayed-module Playwright regression that holds `MarketScreen.jsx`, verifies the frame/header/nav/sidebars remain visible, verifies the market body is still a loader, and verifies `firstScreenReady` stays `false` until the module is released and `market-workspace` renders.

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 14:03 UTC`
- Current request: investigate why account pages are not receiving streamed realtime data and the positions table is out of sync.
- Current status:
  - Reproduced the data-path problem: the fast global broker account SSE can deliver positions, while the heavier account-page SSE can stall before its first `critical` payload under account-page startup load.
  - Root cause in the frontend cache path: `applyIbkrAccountPayloadToCache` only patched account-page positions when a scoped positions response already existed, so the working broker stream could not seed the account page while REST/page SSE was still empty.
  - Fixed the cache seeding path so broker account stream payloads create scoped account-position responses, including rows and totals, before REST/page SSE has populated them.
  - Reduced visible-account startup contention by delaying the inactive account-page SSE until the active account-page stream has delivered a fresh critical payload, and delaying inactive account prewarm until the active account is critical-ready.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
  - `artifacts/pyrus/src/screens/AccountScreen.jsx`
  - `artifacts/pyrus/src/features/platform/live-streams.ts`
  - `artifacts/pyrus/src/features/platform/live-streams.test.ts`
  - `artifacts/pyrus/src/screens/account/accountCalendarData.test.js`

- Last updated: `2026-05-28 00:32 UTC`
- Current request: fix the apparent Replit/Pyrus port issue shown by API stream aborts, Vite proxy hangups, and an API crash from `polygon-delayed-stream.ts`.
- Current status:
  - Patched the immediate API crash. Current evidence says the port failures are secondary fallout after `@workspace/api-server` exits.
  - Immediate crash path: `closeSocket()` in `artifacts/api-server/src/services/polygon-delayed-stream.ts` removes all WebSocket listeners and then closes a socket while it may still be CONNECTING, so `ws` emits `WebSocket was closed before the connection was established` with no `error` listener and Node terminates.
  - Existing Massive stock quote stream already has the safer pattern: keep an error listener during close, terminate CONNECTING sockets, and avoid opening another socket while one is connecting.
  - `polygon-delayed-stream.ts` now mirrors that safe close/refresh behavior and records close-time WebSocket errors instead of letting them crash the process.
  - Live check after patch:
    - Dev app is running under normal `pnpm --filter @workspace/pyrus run dev:replit` process tree.
    - API PID `18363` and Vite PID `18429` remained alive through direct probes and a stock-aggregate SSE open/timeout/abort.
    - `/api/streams/stocks/aggregates?symbols=SPY,QQQ&sessionId=codex-check` emitted `ready` with source `massive-websocket`; the client timed out after `12s`, and API stayed alive.
    - Follow-up probes returned `/api/session` `200` and frontend `/` `200`.
    - Six-sample stability loop over about one minute kept the same API PID alive, `/api/session` at `200`, Polygon provider `ok`, and IBKR stream state `quiet`.
    - Remaining pressure signals: API RSS roughly `1.5GB`, event-loop p95 roughly `466-482ms`; this is separate from the fixed API-exit/port-collapse path.
  - No Replit Run action, app restart, workflow edit, artifact TOML edit, or startup config edit.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/polygon-delayed-stream.ts`

- Current status:
  - Diagnosis-only pass; no production code changes.
  - There are still four loader surfaces in active source:
    - Static pre-React boot loader in `artifacts/pyrus/index.html` with `data-testid="removed-static-boot-id"`.
    - React root Suspense fallback in `artifacts/pyrus/src/app/App.tsx` with `data-testid="removed-root-fallback-id"`.
    - Platform shell Suspense fallback in `PlatformShell.jsx` using `removed screen fallback component` / `removed-screen-fallback-id`.
    - In-component route skeleton in `screenRegistry.jsx` using `removed route shell component` / `removed-route-shell-id-${screenId}`.
  - The strongest page-level duplication is `PlatformShell` wrapping every rendered screen in a Suspense fallback while `createPreloadableScreen()` does not suspend and instead renders `removed route shell component` while its module loads.
  - Source/e2e tests explicitly assert these loader surfaces, especially `LogoLoader.test.ts`, `platformRootSource.test.js`, and `market-responsive.spec.ts`; that is why another agent keeps surfacing them during search.
  - Hidden warm-mounted screens can instantiate the route-shell path, but `ScreenTransitionHost` applies `display: none` when inactive, so the visible surface fight is mainly active route loading plus the redundant fallback systems, not hidden panes visibly overlaying the app.

- Last updated: `2026-05-27 22:46 UTC`
- Current request: align status coloring for the SIGNAL/FLOW/ALGO header lane sine waves.
- Current status:
  - Replaced per-lane ad hoc wave color inputs with one status-driven path in `HeaderBroadcastScrollerStack.jsx`.
  - `HeaderLaneWaveIcon` now canonicalizes a stream status and colors the wave with `streamStateTokenVar(state)`, so identical statuses use identical colors across all three lanes.
  - Shared semantics now apply:
    - `healthy` -> green fast wave.
    - `checking`/capacity/reconnect -> blue/accent slow wave.
    - `stale`/degraded/paused/last-flow -> amber flat wave.
    - `offline` -> red flat wave.
    - `no-subscribers`/`market-closed` -> neutral flat wave.
  - SIGNAL maps error/degraded/busy/on/off into those statuses.
  - FLOW maps error/degraded/stale/paused/quiet/busy/on/off into those statuses.
  - ALGO maps syncing to `checking`, available to `healthy`, and unavailable to `no-subscribers`.
  - Preserved unrelated dirty worktree changes and concurrent handoff entries below this current entry.
  - No Replit Run action, app restart, workflow edit, artifact TOML edit, or startup config edit.

- Last updated: `2026-05-27 22:26 UTC`
- Current request: diagnose the dev-run crash showing slow API requests followed by `WebSocket was closed before the connection was established` in `massive-stock-quote-stream.ts`.
- Current status:
  - Diagnosis: the API process crashed because `closeSocket()` removed all WebSocket listeners and then called `socket.close()` while the Massive stock socket was still connecting. The `ws` package emitted an `error` after the error listener had been removed, which made Node throw an unhandled error and exit.
  - The Vite proxy `socket hang up` lines are downstream symptoms after the API process died.
  - The preceding `level:40` API request logs are slow-request warnings, not the direct crash.
  - Patched `closeSocket()` to detach the socket from global state, keep an error listener attached, and terminate connecting sockets instead of closing them.
  - Patched `refreshSocket()` to leave an in-flight connecting socket alone; auth success subscribes to the current desired symbol set.
  - API dist rebuilt. No Replit Run action, workflow edit, artifact TOML edit, or startup config edit.
  - Post-restart check after normal Replit Run App:
    - API process `88681` and Vite process `88747` are running.
    - `GET /api/session` returns 200 with Massive configured and IBKR connected.
    - Web root on port `18747` returns 200.
    - Shadow option positions API returns both corrected rows: APLD mark `3.579865`, CRWV mark `4.20`.
    - Runtime diagnostics show Massive stock quotes `authState: authenticated`, `connected: true`, `subscribedSymbolCount: 550`, `lastError: null`.
    - A 20-second follow-up still showed the same API PID alive with fresh Massive messages.
  - Remaining issue: resource pressure is still high after boot (`rssMb` around `1880.6`, event-loop p95 around `376 ms`), so the crash is fixed but performance pressure remains.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/massive-stock-quote-stream.ts`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/massive-stock-quote-stream.test.ts`

    - Signal-options automation is contributing a long scan under critical pressure; resource-pressure caps currently show `maintenanceOnly=false` and `skipDeploymentScans=false`, so this pressure does not pause deployment scans by itself.
    - IBKR/watchlist live demand is an amplifier: about `134` active quote subscriptions, `124` equity subscriptions, `10` option subscriptions, and `119` prewarm symbols, mostly `watchlist-prewarm`.
    - SSE/browser reconnect churn is a symptom and amplifier: quotes/accounts/orders/account-page streams repeatedly close with `request_aborted`, and one account-page stream hit a write backpressure timeout.

- Last updated: `2026-05-27 16:04 UTC`
- Current request: review/audit the loader consolidation work to make sure it functions as intended.
- Current status:
  - Audit found the duplicate root logo problem was fixed, but the first browser smoke exposed a short blank root interval after `removed-root-fallback-id` disappeared and before `PlatformShell` committed.
  - Root cause under review: `AppContent` awaited the `PlatformApp` import, but then rendered it through `React.lazy`; even a resolved lazy module can suspend once, and with `fallback={null}` that briefly commits an empty root.
  - Fix direction: keep the no-second-logo behavior, but render the preloaded initial route component directly when the outer boot preload has resolved. Retain the lazy/null fallback only as a defensive path for any caller that renders `AppContent` without awaiting the exported preload.

- Last updated: `2026-05-27 02:38 UTC`
- Current request: audit the Algo right-rail redesign work and make sure it went perfectly.
- Current status:
  - Implemented the first complete right-rail redesign pass.
  - Added global Algo rail container-query utilities (`.algo-rail-cq`, `.algo-settings-grid`, `.algo-diag-kpi-grid`, `.algo-cell--wide`, `.algo-cell--full`, `.tnum`) and pinned them in `uiTokens.test.js`.
  - Replaced the old two-tier settings layout with a single `SETTINGS_SECTIONS` model and render loop. Deleted the unused `SettingsFormRow.jsx` full-size fallback path.
  - Added the Contract custom block with DTE controls and CALL/PUT strike ladder, plus the Exits custom PnL track with marker edit popover. Kept `trailGivebackPct` as a compact cell so no existing editable setting disappears.
  - Reworked `HaltStrip` into an always-expanded board with state pills, group rollups, active/forced left accent bars, and CSS container-query grid classes.
  - Applied diagnostics KPI grid and tabular numeric polish, save-bar value alignment, and section-header spacing polish.
  - Added an opt-in `radioGroup` mode to shared `SegmentedControl` so Algo segmented fields can expose radiogroup/radio semantics without changing the primitive default elsewhere.
  - Live browser smoke initially found a runtime crash in `ExitLadderTrack` (`marker.positionValue` numeric/function collision); fixed and reran smoke successfully.
  - Audit pass found and fixed three additional issues:
    - At the `<300px` container-query breakpoint, `.algo-cell--wide` still spanned two implicit grid columns because the global span rule won the cascade. Moved the one-column override after the base rule and pinned it in `uiTokens.test.js`.
    - Strike ladder ArrowUp/ArrowDown moved through the raw ascending slot list instead of the descending visual ladder. Added a visual-order slot list so ArrowUp moves to the row above and ArrowDown moves to the row below.
    - Strike ladder ARIA/dirty state was incomplete: each row was its own radiogroup and CALL/PUT header dirty dots were missing. Reworked the ladder so each CALL/PUT column is one radiogroup with six radios and header dirty dots appear when the corresponding strike slot is edited.
  - Audit also found a narrow exit-track overflow from edge marker labels. Added inset-aware marker positioning so min/max labels stay inside the track at forced narrow widths.
  - Current worktree has many unrelated prior-session edits; preserve them and avoid reverting unrelated files.

- Current request: implement the IBKR data-line / historical-request stabilization plan, grounded in IBKR API pacing docs.
- Current status:
  - Implemented first stability slice: added `ibkr-historical-admission.ts`, routed platform historical bars through it, lowered signal monitor/matrix caps, made trade monitor worker use capped profiles, stopped signal-options force refresh from bypassing caps, exposed historical admission in line usage, and added Settings rows for historical API/bridge pressure.

  - Live `/api/settings/ibkr-line-usage` shows historical admission active and protecting the signal-matrix caller: `concurrency=1`, `queueCap=2`, `accepted=54`, `active=0`, `queued=0`, `lastRejectedReason=global-historical-pacing`, `globalWindowRemainingWeight=0/54`.
  - Live Settings > Data & Broker renders the new historical rows after the SSE snapshot arrives; first render can show placeholders, then values populate at about 3 seconds.
  - Follow-up review requested for signal scanner cadence because watchlist signal bubbles are not all hydrated. Live state: signal profile `paper` is enabled, timeframe `5m`, poll `60s`, max symbols `60`, concurrency `2`; all watchlists contain `90` unique symbols; profile state currently resolves `60` and reports historical lane dropping `30`.
  - Current 2m/5m/15m matrix cadence is intentionally partial: frontend requests at most `8` symbols per poll under normal pressure (`4` priority + `4` rotating background), backend caps matrix to `8` symbols and concurrency `1`, and historical admission caps the family to `54` weighted requests per 10 minutes.

- Last updated: `2026-05-27 00:18 UTC`
- Current request: watch and confirm whether the Algo monitor sidebar is refetching/resetting every few seconds.
- Current status:
  - Confirmed in a headless Chromium runtime probe on the live dev app (`127.0.0.1:18747`) that the desktop right Algo Monitor sidebar resets while sitting on the Algo screen.
  - In a ~70s observation window, the sidebar rendered real signal/action data, then flipped back to `Preparing algo monitor / Waiting for the active section to finish first paint` around `46.1s`, `58.1s`, and `68.3s`, before data returned.
  - Network trace did not show a tight REST refetch loop. The configured sidebar fallback polls are `30s`, while Algo page fallback queries use `QUERY_DEFAULTS.refetchInterval` (`15s`) when cockpit SSE freshness is not available.
  - The reset text is rendered only when `PlatformAlgoMonitorSidebar` receives `queryEnabled=false`, which comes from `dataEnabled={algoFrameRuntimeEnabled}` in `PlatformShell`.
  - Likely source: `algoFrameRuntimeEnabled` depends on `frameAuxiliaryDataEnabled`, which depends on the active screen's `backgroundAllowed` readiness. `AlgoScreen` sets `backgroundAllowed` from `derivedReady`; `derivedReady` drops whenever `algoFullFresh` expires and the 6s derived REST fallback timer has not elapsed. Because stream freshness is only considered fresh for `7s`, intermittent Algo cockpit SSE/full updates can create a repeated off window and make the sidebar look like it is refetching/remounting every few seconds.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
- Validation state:
  - Runtime watched via Playwright-style headless Chromium script; no product code edited.
  - Source inspected: `PlatformAlgoMonitorSidebar.jsx`, `PlatformShell.jsx`, `PlatformApp.jsx`, `AlgoScreen.jsx`, `live-streams.ts`, and query defaults.
- Next step:
  - Fix should focus on the readiness/runtime gate rather than indicator settings: make the active Algo frame/sidebar data gate not drop after first derived readiness, or make `backgroundAllowed`/Algo derived readiness sticky while the Algo screen remains mounted.

- Last updated: `2026-05-27 00:06 UTC`
- Current request: second look at the indicator's wait-for-bar-close setting as a possible cause of Algo/monitor signal timing and jumping.
- Current status:
  - Corrected/expanded finding: `waitForBarClose` is important here. Core default is `waitForBarClose=true`; the current live `pyrusSignalsSettings` does not override it, so the monitor is running with wait-for-close enabled.
  - Server monitor evaluates with `includeProvisionalSignals: false` and also filters to completed bars. In `evaluatePyrusSignalsSignals`, a signal is actionable only when `includeProvisionalSignals || !waitForBarClose || index < chartBars.length - 1`.
  - Because the server already removes partial bars, `waitForBarClose=true` effectively delays newest-completed-bar signals until one additional completed bar exists. New signals therefore first appear with `barsSinceSignal=1`, not `0`.
  - This does contribute to the observed timing/reordering: at each 5m bar edge, previously hidden signals become visible together, while older names cross the `freshWindowBars=8` cutoff. The mixed-generation state publication still explains why different surfaces/rows can disagree momentarily.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
- Validation state:
  - Source inspected: `waitForBarClose` resolver/default, core `actionable` logic, signal monitor completed-bar filtering, and server monitor `includeProvisionalSignals:false` calls.
- Next step:
  - If fixing, decide whether server monitor should treat completed-bar input differently for `waitForBarClose` so alerts fire at candle close instead of one bar later, and separately stabilize monitor state publication by scan generation.

- Last updated: `2026-05-27 00:02 UTC`
- Current request: review the underlying indicator settings driving the Algo/monitor signals and determine whether those settings explain the fresh/not-fresh jumping.
- Current status:
  - Current live signal monitor profile is permissive: `timeframe=5m`, `freshWindowBars=8`, `pollIntervalSeconds=60`, `maxSymbols=250`, `evaluationConcurrency=10`, `pyrusSignalsSettings={ timeHorizon: 8, bosConfirmation: "wicks", chochAtrBuffer: 0, chochBodyExpansionAtr: 0, chochVolumeGate: 0, __signalMonitorUniverseScope: "all_watchlists" }`.
  - Deployment parameters match the profile: `signalTimeframe=5m`, `timeHorizon=8`, `bosConfirmation=wicks`, CHOCH gates all `0`, 90-symbol universe.
  - Core defaults fill omitted values with filters disabled (`signalFiltersEnabled=false`, `requireMtf* / requireAdx / requireVolScoreRange=false`, `restrictToSelectedSessions=false`, `waitForBarClose=true`).
  - Conclusion: these settings explain why many symbols can produce signals, including after-hours/session-labeled signals, but they are not the direct cause of the UI jumping between fresh/not-fresh. The direct cause remains per-row freshness (`barsSinceSignal <= freshWindowBars`) updating across mixed latest-bar generations.

- Current status:
  - Deeper inspection found the latest startup command path is mostly guarded: `.replit` points Run to `artifacts/pyrus: web`, the artifact command traps `HUP` and runs `pnpm --filter @workspace/pyrus run dev:replit`, and duplicate Replit starts no-op inside the startup guard window. No latest log evidence showed `EADDRINUSE`, root `.replit` runner misuse, or a duplicate workflow restart cascade.

  - Current signal monitor profile is aggressive again: enabled all-watchlists paper profile, `90` resolved symbols, `pollIntervalSeconds=60`, `maxSymbols=250`, `evaluationConcurrency=10`.
  - Code-level creator path: `SIGNAL_MONITOR_EVALUATION_PRESSURE_CAPS` currently leaves normal/watch/high/critical profile evaluations at `250` symbols and concurrency `10`; commit `fcf9106` raised this from prior concurrency `3` to `10` and updated tests to assert preserved breadth under pressure.
  - Additional creator path: `startTradeMonitorWorker()` runs immediately after API `listen` and its worker uses raw `profile.maxSymbols` / `profile.evaluationConcurrency`, not `cappedSignalMonitorEvaluationProfile`; it can therefore run a 90-symbol signal monitor pass while the UI matrix and startup streams are also warming.
  - Frontend creator path: `PlatformApp.jsx` starts `runSignalMatrixEvaluation()` immediately when `signalMatrixRuntimeReady` is true, then repeats at the profile poll interval. The request planner sends priority symbols before background readiness, and the API evaluates three matrix timeframes (`2m`, `5m`, `15m`), so even the matrix cap of 16 symbols means up to 48 bar evaluations per call plus stale/live-edge retries.
  - Bridge/watchlist pressure remains an amplifier: `startIbkrWatchlistPrewarmRuntime()` reconciles at startup and every `15s`; latest workflow logs show quote bootstrap HTTP `500`, market-subscription lane `504`, order snapshot timeouts, and many SSE aborts while matrix calls are running.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 23:56 UTC`
- Current request: diagnose why the Algo page and monitor jump between showing names as updated since VST and not updated since VST.
- Current status:
  - Root cause found: the Algo page and monitor both ultimately read `/api/signal-monitor/state`, but the response can contain symbol rows from different per-symbol evaluation moments/latest bars. Freshness is computed per row as `barsSinceSignal <= freshWindowBars` and not stale, so as individual symbols advance from the 23:45 UTC completed bar to the 23:50 UTC completed bar, the fresh/not-fresh boundary and row order move.
  - Live API sample at 23:53-23:56 UTC showed profile `timeframe=5m`, `freshWindowBars=8`, `maxSymbols=250`, universe 90 symbols, and rows mixed across `latestBarAt=2026-05-26T23:45:00.000Z` and `2026-05-26T23:50:00.000Z`.
  - `VST` specifically moved from `barsSinceSignal=4`, `latestBarAt=23:45` to `barsSinceSignal=5`, `latestBarAt=23:50`; adjacent names were on different bar edges during the same sampled monitor response.
  - Algo uses cockpit/state signals from `getSignalMonitorState`; monitor/activity publishes and renders the same signal monitor state plus events. They are not pinned to one coherent scan generation, so both surfaces can show the same transition at slightly different times.
  - No app code changes made in this pass.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
- Validation state:
  - Source inspected: signal monitor service, signal-options automation bridge, Algo UI, PlatformApp signal monitor queries, Market activity lane.
  - Runtime inspected through local API calls against the running Replit dev app on `127.0.0.1:8080`.
- Next step:
  - If fixing, choose whether to stabilize UI by scan generation/bar edge, defer row replacement until a full monitor cycle completes, or explicitly label mixed-generation states as refreshing instead of letting the fresh boundary jump.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 21:12 UTC`
- Current request: take a first-principles approach to page load/render slowness and check whether something prevents the Algo page from rendering while in viewport.
- Current status:
  - Re-diagnosed from browser timing instead of route fallback only.
  - Found the main first-principles issue: dynamic screen/module requests share the dev app origin with large API/SSE requests, so background preloads and Algo data requests can occupy browser connection slots and delay code needed for the visible page.
  - Changed background screen/research preloads to require an observed normal memory state; they no longer start from the default "normal before measured" assumption.
  - Removed registry-time boot preloading and moved screen preloading to explicit nav intent plus safe idle gates.
  - Fixed the Algo blank viewport path: `AlgoLivePage` is preloaded as soon as `AlgoScreen` loads, heavy Algo streams/queries wait until the live page module is ready, the live area has a real fallback instead of `null`, and the signal table/positions/drill modules are deferred behind local Suspense boundaries so the operations frame can paint first.
  - Browser validation now shows direct Algo load renders `algo-live-grid` without pressing Run Scan; the signal table follows after its lazy module resolves. Remaining slowness is dev/API connection contention and heavy table/positions chunks, not a blank viewport gate.
- Changed files this pass:

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 20:12 UTC`
- Current request: monitor/audit the rendering-policy changes because pages still feel like they make the user wait.
- Current status:
  - Live source audit complete. The running Vite app is serving the latest rendering-policy changes for Market, Flow, Trade, and Backtest.
  - The remaining visible waits are primarily above/beside those fixes: global boot has a lazy waterfall (`AppContent` then `PlatformApp`), screen modules are lazy-loaded on first navigation, and background screen preloading starts only after first screen ready plus the configured 750ms stagger.
  - HTTP timing from the running dev server shows expensive first screen chunks: `FlowScreen.jsx` about 1.0MB/317ms, `TradeScreen.jsx` about 597KB/916ms, `BacktestingPanels.tsx` about 1.18MB/317ms, `SettingsScreen.jsx` about 635KB/191ms, `MultiChartGrid.jsx` about 236KB/107ms, and `TradeOrderTicket.jsx` about 419KB/121ms.
  - Core API timings were not the main bottleneck in this sample: `/api/session` about 2ms, `/api/watchlists` about 287ms, `/api/signal-monitor/state` about 135ms, `/api/flow/events` about 3ms, `/api/algo/deployments` about 2ms.
  - Playwright browser timing was blocked by the container missing `libgbm.so.1`, so this audit used served-source checks and HTTP/module timing instead of a real browser waterfall.
  - Follow-up headless audit now works by running Chromium with Nix `mesa-libgbm` in `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`; no Replit startup config or repo dependency files were changed.
  - Browser audit confirmed the real visible wait: after an 8s idle warmup on Market, clicking Flow showed the route-level `removed-screen-fallback-id` after about 500ms and it was still visible after 9s, even though the header/sidebar were live and the warmup snapshot claimed screen code preload was complete.
  - The warmup snapshot only had `screenReadiness.market`; Flow never reported readiness because the active route was still suspended. `screenCodePreloadCompleteAtMs` is timer-based, not actual import completion.
  - Memory pressure was `critical`, so hidden screen warm-mount was disabled, but code preloading still ran. This means we preload screen code but do not guarantee active route modules are ready before navigation.
  - First-pass cold Flow navigation also reproduced a long route fallback: visible around 236ms after click and still not gone after the 20s wait window.
  - Preserve the staged consolidation work already present in the tree; do not stage, commit, or touch Replit startup config during this audit.

  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`

  - Passed: headless Chromium launch after adding Nix `mesa-libgbm` to `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`.
  - Passed: browser audit reproduced route-level Flow fallback persisting after both cold-fast and warmed navigation.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 19:17 UTC`
- Current request: review slow IBKR deactivate behavior and keep Gateway shutdown reliable.
- Current status:
  - User confirmed deactivate eventually worked, but took too long.
  - Root cause in the previous helper-ownership fix: launch jobs should stay with the effective v23 helper, but shutdown jobs must be claimable by any authenticated helper for that desktop because the long-running polling agent may still be older while v23 was only the recent one-click launcher.
  - Patched remote job claiming so older duplicate helpers still cannot steal launch jobs, but can claim shutdown jobs immediately; shutdown URLs still self-update helpers as needed.
  - Patched shutdown creation to delete unclaimed jobs for the same desktop so Deactivate cannot leave a pending launch job that restarts the bridge after shutdown.
  - API bundle rebuilt; running backend needs normal Replit restart to load this latest shutdown-claim behavior.

  - Watchlist line usage is live and no longer `0 reserved`: `/api/settings/ibkr-line-usage` at `2026-05-26T16:37:29.367Z` showed visible pool `120/120`, watchlist prewarm `sourceSymbolCount=90`, `primaryActiveSourceSymbolCount=90`, `primaryMissingSourceSymbolCount=0`, `recentRejectedCount=0`, and `fillerEnabled=false`.
  - Signal-options state is live: `/api/algo/deployments/paper-enabled/signal-options/state` returned 200 with `signalCount=40`, `freshSignalCount=1`, latest signal `RBLX buy signalAt=2026-05-26T16:25:00.000Z latestBarAt=2026-05-26T16:30:00.000Z fresh=true`, `candidateCount=75`, `selectedContractCandidateCount=16`, and `activePositionContractCount=2`.
  - Algo worker is automatically rotating, not off: cockpit `scan_universe` first reported waiting with last batch `16/90` and next due, then later reported `running`, then advanced to next batch `TLT, IEF, UUP, GLD, USO, AMZN, META, GOOGL` with `lastBatchSize=8`, `lastBatchStartIndex=16`, `lastBatchNextIndex=24`, `nextScanDueAt=2026-05-26T16:40:01.378Z`. Backend now exposes batch/due/running/pressure fields, and the Algo signal table now displays the cockpit scan detail when available instead of reducing waiting/running scans to vague timestamps.
  - Final live signal-options sample at `2026-05-26T16:45Z` showed `lastSignalAt=2026-05-26T16:30:00.000Z`, `freshSignals=2`, latest fresh signals `COHR buy 16:30Z` and `RBLX buy 16:25Z`, `candidateCount=75`, and `selectedContracts=16`.
  - Signal matrix/watchlist-dot coverage is live: POST `/api/signal-monitor/matrix` for 10 visible symbols returned `stateCount=10`, `truncated=false`, `skippedSymbols=[]` in `1786ms`. The matrix no longer drops visible requests under pressure; delayed intraday bars are marked stale with `Latest signal monitor bar is delayed; waiting for live broker history.` instead of being labeled fresh.
  - Root crash payload root cause was a retained Trade lazy import resolving to `undefined`: `TradeScreen` expected `MiniChartTickerSearch`, but the ticker search module only exported `MarketChartTickerSearch`. `MiniChartTickerSearch` is exported again and `lazyWithRetry` now throws a labeled error if a dynamic import resolves without a default component.
  - Browser diagnostics after the 5-minute cutoff showed `eventCount5m=0`, `criticalCount5m=0`, `lastEventAt=null`, and `recentEvents=[]`. Overall diagnostics remain `degraded` from slow client API timings, especially `/api/signal-monitor/matrix`, not from a current root crash.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 16:10 UTC`
- Current request: investigate and fix watchlist sidebar signal dots so they are populated and aligned with the chart signal state.
- Current status:
  - Root causes confirmed:
    - The chart Pyrus Signals default was still the old Pine-style `timeHorizon=10`, `bosConfirmation=close`, while the signal monitor/algo profile feeding watchlist dots is tuned to `timeHorizon=8`, `bosConfirmation=wicks`. AAPL 5m direct source comparison showed an actual mismatch: profile/dot settings produced latest `sell` at `13:25Z`, while chart-default settings produced latest `buy` at `14:05Z`.
    - The frontend matrix request planner tried to send priority plus background symbols, but the backend hard-capped normal matrix work to 8 symbols. A 10-symbol source probe returned only 24 states, skipped `PLTR`/`COIN`, and reported `truncated=true`, so background/overflow sidebar dots could not populate.
  - Patched chart defaults and persisted legacy chart defaults so charts now align with the tuned signal-monitor profile: default/migrated chart settings use `timeHorizon=8`, `bosConfirmation=wicks`.
  - Patched the matrix scheduler so active watchlist symbols are priority, normal requests reserve room for background rotation under a 16-symbol request budget, and pressure budgets stay bounded.
  - Patched backend signal matrix pressure caps to match the frontend request budget: normal 16, watch 12, high 8, critical 4. This prevents the server from silently dropping the background symbols the frontend requested.
  - HTTP after Replit restart now proves the earlier signal-options 500 is fixed: `/api/algo/deployments/paper-enabled/signal-options/state` returned 200. `/api/signal-monitor/state?environment=paper` shows current 5m live state at latest `16:00Z`. HTTP `/api/signal-monitor/matrix` with 10 symbols x 1 timeframe returned all 10 states, `skippedSymbols=[]`, `truncated=false`.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 15:54 UTC`
- Current request: continue the IBKR data-line/runtime audit and incorporate the fresh-signal diagnosis: IB Gateway live bars must feed signal monitor/algo signals instead of accepting 15-minute-delayed Massive bars; `/api/algo/deployments/paper-enabled/signal-options/state` must stop returning 500.
- Current status:
  - Watchlist line-usage goal is now proven live after restart: sampled `/api/settings/ibkr-line-usage` at `2026-05-26T15:39:35Z`; 90 watchlist symbols, `primarySymbolLimit=120`, `primaryActiveSourceSymbolCount=90`, `primaryMissingSourceSymbolCount=0`, no rejected watchlist intersections, and frontend runtime model renders `Watchlist Quotes: 120 active of 120 reserved`.
  - Live signal diagnosis was confirmed: existing signal events showed `signalAt` / `latestBarAt` lagging by roughly 15-20 minutes, and the HTTP signal-options state route returned 500 because `paper-enabled` was passed directly into a UUID query.
  - Patched `loadSignalMonitorCompletedBars` so intraday delayed bars (`delayed: true`, `freshness: "delayed"`, delayed market-data mode, Massive/Polygon delayed source) force broker live-edge retry before the stale-age window. If live broker bars still cannot replace them, the state is degraded/stale and cannot be marked `fresh: true`. Daily bars keep existing delayed/historical behavior.
  - Removed `retryStale: false` from signal matrix evaluation so matrix rows also get the live-edge retry/degrade semantics instead of being locked to delayed snapshots.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 15:29 UTC`
- Current request: implement the rendering-policy performance plan for slow page/container loading.
- Current status:
  - Implemented the rendering-policy fix. Root cause was a stale hidden-screen warm-mount path in `PlatformApp` that ignored `appWorkScheduler`'s `hiddenScreenPreload.mountScreens: false`, so retained pages could still be idle-mounted after warmup even when policy said not to mount them.
  - Removed the one-frame `requestAnimationFrame` gate from active-screen data activation in `PlatformScreenRouter`; the selected screen now starts its data work immediately.
  - Added an idle fallback to `DeferredRender` so below-fold panels still mount after idle even if `IntersectionObserver` never fires, while keeping the intersection fast path.
  - Updated source-contract coverage so the scheduler gate, immediate active-screen data flags, and deferred-panel idle fallback are guarded.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 15:14 UTC`
- Current request: user reports the shadow account heatmap `% return` still jumps back and forth; thoroughly audit all involved account page data paths and patch the real remaining cause.
- Current status:
  - Root cause pinpointed in the Today shadow heatmap path, not only the P&L calendar/equity-history cache path. `TodaySnapshotPanel` uses `PositionTreemapPanel` with `useLiveOptionPositionRows`; the frontend live option quote overlay was overwriting shadow-ledger `dayChange` / `dayChangePercent` with quote-session day change while backend shadow positions intentionally prefer ledger daily-baseline day change. Account-page stream payloads then restored the backend value, creating the visible back-and-forth.
  - Patched `PositionsPanel.jsx` so shadow ledger rows preserve backend day-change fields whenever the backend supplied them, while still accepting live quote mark, market value, unrealized P&L, greeks, and quote-display updates.
  - Kept the earlier cache ownership fix for performance-calendar equity: selected range equity history and the calendar's `1Y` history now use separate React Query keys, and derived stream writes preserve newer live terminal equity points.
  - Full API unit validation also exposed a nearby live-data guard failure: watchlist prewarm had silently opted into `fallbackProvider: "polygon"` despite the account/live-flow default requiring explicit Polygon opt-in. Patched that admission path back to `fallbackProvider: "cache"` without touching the broader pre-existing line-budget refactor.

- Current request: monitor live runtime for 5 minutes because watchlist prices must update realtime and `watchlist=0` in line usage is suspicious.
- Current status:

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 15:04 UTC`
- Current request: audit the latest line-allocation implementation because it degraded realtime watchlist/scanner/algo behavior.
- Current status:
  - Audit found a real regression in the last allocation change: default visible/watchlist prewarm was capped at `60` lines while the 200-line app budget still had idle global capacity, so a 90-symbol visible set rejected 30 live quote lines before scanner work even started.
  - Fixed default visible capacity to reserve scanner headroom without hard-reserving idle account/execution capacity. Default budget is now visible `120`, flow scanner `80`, watchlist filler `0`; small budgets keep a visible floor so live warm-up is not accidentally capped to zero.
  - Fixed a priority enforcement hole affecting algo contract quotes: `signal-options` quote leases already had higher priority, but admission could not use it to preempt lower-priority scanner/global lines or lower-priority automation lines inside the Algo & Execution bundle. Those leases can now reclaim lower-priority work while still not allowing visible lines to reclaim signal-options maintenance lines.
  - Verified with a direct admission probe: 90 visible prewarm lines and 80 scanner lines are both admitted under the default 200-line budget, leaving 30 global lines unused instead of rejecting visible quotes.

- Last updated: `2026-05-26 13:53 UTC`
- Current request: check mobile menus for proper function; chart indicator menu is symptomatic of broader menu/touch issues.
- Current status:
  - Root cause found in the shared tooltip wrapper: `AppTooltip` recognized direct interactive elements like `<button>`, but did not recognize Radix `asChild` triggers that contain a button. On touch/pen devices, the tooltip could therefore intercept first pointer-down for dropdown/popover menus.
  - Implemented a shared fix: `AppTooltip` now recursively detects interactive descendants under `asChild` triggers and skips first-tap tooltip gating for those menu triggers.
  - This covers chart settings/chart type desktop-style Radix dropdowns, header popovers, and similar wrapped triggers. The chart indicator control still uses a phone bottom sheet when `isPhone` is true, and the same direct-button path remains covered.
  - Worktree already has unrelated dirty files from concurrent/prior work; preserve them.

- Last updated: `2026-05-25 15:19 UTC`
- Current request: reassess whether Replit startup files need to change to fully stop the persistent Run console crashes/disconnects.
- Current status:
  - Official Replit docs reviewed after the user asked to avoid guessing:
    - Replit App configuration docs say `.replit` controls app behavior and the `run` property is the command used by the Run button when configured; customized/complex apps can use Workflows.
    - Workflows docs say a workflow can be assigned to the Run button and replacing the default run behavior is controlled by the selected Run-button workflow; choosing default "Run Replit App" keeps the default `.replit` run command behavior.
    - Console docs say each Run-button use creates a Console entry showing the command, timestamps, stdout, and stderr.
    - Ports docs say `[[ports]]` maps local ports to public external ports and warns that mismatched listened ports versus configured ports are a common failure mode.
    - Older-project artifact migration docs say ports and workflows must match the expected default configuration for the project type; manual port/workflow edits can produce `workflow config does not match expected`.
    - Usage limits docs say CPU/RAM are hard limits determined by plan, and excessive/constrained network activity can make operations fail or the app temporarily unavailable.
  - Rechecked the current Replit startup path. `.replit` has no root `run`, `[agent] stack = "PNPM_WORKSPACE"`, and `[workflows] runButton = "artifacts/pyrus: web"`. `artifacts/pyrus/.replit-artifact/artifact.toml` still uses `pnpm --filter @workspace/pyrus run dev:replit`.
  - `pnpm run audit:replit-startup` passed, and `pnpm run replit:config:status` confirmed `.replit`, `replit.nix`, and `artifacts/pyrus/.replit-artifact/artifact.toml` are locked read-only.
  - Latest duplicate workflow logs (`z0YBu5THXnRSS669ftgUW`, `wU3WZtRtcFPPK8Q7OWa3J`) prove duplicate Run events exit with the supervisor duplicate no-op and do not restart API/web.
  - Latest real workflow log (`stwP8KucRM5DoiqlPV3R7`) shows a normal artifact startup: API build, API health, then Vite. No `SIGTERM`, `SIGKILL`, `EADDRINUSE`, or `reapDevPort` startup churn was found in that log.
  - Live API PID `7599` started at `2026-05-25 14:51 UTC`; the holiday/session-status rebuild happened later, so the running process still reports `strictReason=stream_not_fresh` on Memorial Day until the next Replit Run restart.
  - Found one source gap that would survive restart: `bridge-quote-stream.ts` had its own weekday/time `isLikelyUsEquitySession()` without full-day holidays. That stream watchdog could still reconnect/log quote stream stalls on Memorial Day even after the session-status path became holiday-aware.

- Last updated: `2026-05-25 14:47 UTC`
- Current request: continue investigating until the persistent Replit terminal/run-console disconnect cause is pinpointed.
- Pinpointed issue:
  - The app is not primarily crashing because Replit starts the artifact instead of a root workflow. The current live API process stayed up while the console/workspace degraded.
  - The live failure mode is API saturation: before this patch API PID `4319` had RSS around `2.37GB`, CPU around `74%`, event-loop max delay above `10s`, diagnostics calls timing out at `8s`, and `/proc/pressure/io` showed sustained high I/O pressure while cgroup `memory.events` still showed no OOM kill.
  - The creator path is background options-flow/IBKR market-data work continuing after the IBKR quote lane is already unhealthy:
    - `fetchBridgeOptionQuoteSnapshots()` classified `flow-scanner-live` as quote-lane work but also set `bypassBackoff: true`, so flow-scanner quote hydration ignored quote governor backoff.
    - `getOptionsFlowScannerBackgroundBlockReason()` did not consider quote-lane backoff or API resource pressure, so radar/deep scanner work continued to enqueue.
    - The scanner's 45s timeout logs `Options flow scanner timed out scanning ...`, but the underlying option-chain/quote bridge requests are not cancelled by that timeout because the platform fetch path ignores the abort signal. That creates the continuing drain that makes diagnostics and the terminal feel disconnected.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/platform.ts`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/bridge-option-quote-stream.ts`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/options-flow-scanner.test.ts`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/bridge-option-quote-stream.test.ts`
  - `artifacts/api-server/src/services/watchlist-prewarm.test.ts`
- Code changes:
  - Background options-flow scanner now pauses when the quotes lane is backed off (`quotes-backoff`) and when API pressure/RSS is high enough to shed background work (`resource-pressure`).
  - Flow-scanner live option quote snapshots no longer bypass quote governor backoff. They still use the quotes lane instead of the option-chain lane, so they remain unaffected by option-chain governor backoff, but they stop adding quote work during quote outages.
  - Watchlist bridge prewarm sync still skips stale/quote-backed-off states at debug instead of warning and retrying into the same failure.
- Validation state:
  - `pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server exec node --import tsx --test --test-name-pattern "quotes lane is backed off|quote failures do not open|critical API pressure|skips fan-out" src/services/options-flow-scanner.test.ts` passed 4/4.
  - `pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server exec node --import tsx --test src/services/bridge-option-quote-stream.test.ts` passed 15/15.
  - `pnpm --filter @workspace/api-server exec node --import tsx --test src/services/watchlist-prewarm.test.ts` passed 14/14.

- Last updated: `2026-05-25 02:18 UTC`
- Current request: review/audit the IBKR connection process after the Windows PowerShell helper logged progress callback timeouts immediately after `starting_gateway` / `gateway_process_started`.
- Current status:
  - Audited the remote desktop launch, one-time credential handoff, Windows helper progress callback, IB Gateway start/window-detection path, and browser credential delivery path.
  - The pasted `Activation progress callback skipped: The operation has timed out` line comes from `Send-BridgeProgress` after the helper has already written the local progress message. That callback POST to `/api/ibkr/activation/:activationId/progress` uses `Invoke-RestMethod -TimeoutSec 5` and is caught/logged as non-fatal.
  - The pasted sequence proves the helper launched, registered the desktop agent, received encrypted credentials, selected the UI one-time login path, and requested `ibgateway.exe` startup. It does not prove the launch failed at the callback timeout.
  - After `gateway_process_started`, the helper waits 3 seconds, then waits up to 45 seconds for an IB Gateway login window before typing credentials. If the local log does not continue to `typing_gateway_credentials` or `waiting_2fa`, the likely blocker is Gateway window discovery/focus/startup, not credential handoff.
  - Static review found one operator-risk issue: progress callbacks can time out repeatedly during Gateway startup and hide useful browser-side state even though the local helper continues. This should be handled as degraded telemetry, not as a launch failure.
  - PowerShell syntax validation was not available in this Linux workspace because `pwsh` is not installed.
- Changed files this pass:
  - `SESSION_HANDOFF_CURRENT.md`

If no local line appears for more than 45-60 seconds, inspect whether IB Gateway opened off-screen/minimized, whether a Java security/update dialog is in front, or whether `ibgateway.exe` was started under a non-interactive desktop session.

- Current status:
